usages: Relief of mild to moderate pain (headache, toothache, muscle ache, backache) and reduction of fever.
sideEffects: Rarely causes side effects at therapeutic doses. Extremely high doses can lead to severe, irreversible liver toxicity.
precautions: Strictly avoid alcohol. Do not combine with other acetaminophen-containing cough, cold, or sinus medications.

usages: Broadly used in India for relieving severe fever, headache, body aches, and joint discomfort.
sideEffects: Very safe when taken as directed. Overdosage causes severe hepatic necrosis (liver injury).
precautions: Maximum daily dose is 4000mg. Do not repeat dose within 4 hours.

usages: Analgesic and antipyretic suspension/tablet widely used to manage pediatric fevers and mild childhood pain.
sideEffects: Generally safe. Occasionally causes mild nausea, skin rash, or sweating in sensitive individuals.
precautions: Measure liquid doses precisely using a syringe or dosing spoon. Keep away from pediatric reach.

usages: Combination pain reliever formulated for fast relief of headaches, migraines, and severe dental toothaches.
sideEffects: Mild insomnia or restlessness due to caffeine content. Gastric irritation if taken on empty stomach.
precautions: Avoid other caffeinated drinks (tea, coffee) while taking this. Maximum 4 tablets in 24 hours.

usages: Macrolide antibiotic used to treat acute bacterial sinusitis, community-acquired pneumonia, strep throat, and skin infections.
sideEffects: Mild diarrhea, loose stools, abdominal cramping, nausea, or headache. Rare QT interval prolongation on ECG.
precautions: Complete the full antibiotic cycle. Take 1 hour before or 2 hours after meals to maximize absorption.

usages: Beta-lactam penicillin-class antibiotic for middle ear infections, tonsillitis, dental abscesses, and urinary tract infections.
sideEffects: Diarrhea, skin rash, oral thrush, or mild nausea. Can cause severe anaphylactic allergic reactions in some.
precautions: Do not take if allergic to penicillins or cephalosporins. Can reduce birth control effectiveness.

usages: Co-amoxiclav antibiotic targeting beta-lactamase producing bacteria in severe sinus, lung, UTI, and bite wounds.
sideEffects: Diarrhea (extremely common), stomach upset, vaginal itching, or mild skin rashes.
precautions: Take at the start of a meal to reduce gastrointestinal side effects. Report watery diarrhea immediately.

usages: Nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug used to manage dental pain, menstrual cramps, high fever, and rheumatoid arthritis.
sideEffects: Heartburn, indigestion, gastric ulcers, potential GI bleeding, renal strain, or elevated blood pressure.
precautions: Take with food, milk, or antacids. Do not combine with aspirin or warfarin due to bleeding risk.

usages: Second-generation antihistamine offering relief from seasonal allergies, hay fever, hives, and allergic rhinitis.
sideEffects: Mild drowsiness, dry mouth, throat soreness, fatigue, or headache.
precautions: Avoid driving or operating heavy machinery if drowsiness is felt. Restrict concurrent alcohol use.

usages: Proton pump inhibitor (PPI) designed to treat acid reflux, GERD, Zollinger-Ellison syndrome, and stomach ulcers.
sideEffects: Headache, diarrhea, nausea, stomach flatulence, joint stiffness, or dry mouth.
precautions: Take 30-60 minutes before the first meal of the day. Avoid long-term continuous use without supervision.

usages: Reduces stomach acid, helping heal esophagus damage caused by acid reflux and protecting against ulcers.
sideEffects: Abdominal pain, flatulence, nausea, diarrhea, or low magnesium levels after prolonged usage.
precautions: Swallow whole; do not crush or chew. Take in the morning on an empty stomach.

usages: Glucocorticoid steroid used to suppress intense inflammation in asthma, severe allergies, and autoimmune flare-ups.
sideEffects: Increased appetite, rapid weight gain, high blood sugar, mood changes, insomnia, and bone thinning over time.
precautions: Must tap/wean off gradually; never stop abruptly. Can mask signs of active infections.
